title: agentify-finding
description:
Canonical schema for every machine-or-human audit finding in the marketplace (mkt-self-improve, mkt-feedback-triage, mkt-practice-evolve, mkt-audit-trend, feedback-ingest, target-side /

-self-improve). v2 is a BREAKING redesign of the prior plugins/agentify/audit-review-schema.json (v1): the verdict and severity vocabularies are reshaped (`ship|iterate|park|stalled|regression|failure` -> `healthy|degraded|broken`; `strategic` severity replaced by `info`), `description` is renamed to optional `details`, and several optional v1 keys (audit_inputs, caused_by_prior_revise, feedback_issue_id) are dropped from the permitted set. Existing audits/*.md files must be migrated via `bin/migrate-audits-v1-to-v2.sh` (see decisions/0001-finding-schema-unification.md). Every finding still requires (a) at least one fetched URL reference, (b) a falsifiable acceptance criterion, and (c) the WS-F-003 synthetic-source marker when machine-produced.

schema_version.description: finding-schema.json version. Bumped on breaking change. v1 was audit-review-schema.json (kept for backward compatibility); v2 widens producer set.

audit_id.description: Unique identifier for this audit run (typically ISO timestamp or UUID).

synthetic_source.description: WS-F-003: marks the authorship class. Synthetic sources require human-review gate per REVISE_AGENTIFY_PROMPT.md before applying.

acceptance_criterion.description: A falsifiable check — a grep, a CI job, a script — that proves the finding is resolved.

adoption_check_command.description: Optional one-line shell predicate (exit 0 = adopted, 1 = not_adopted, 2 = partial). Used by /mkt-practice-evolve to track recommendation adoption.
